summary: Public SSR site E2E review (frontend only, no admin login). Verified all clean URLs are server-rendered HTML with H1, canonical, JSON-LD, real <a href> menu links (no '#anchor'), 404 for non-existing episode/team slugs, transcript page rendered, mobile <details> menu present, admin SPA still loads, and brand spelling always 'UnoXdue'. Created pytest suite /app/backend/tests/test_public_ssr.py with 40 tests: 39 PASS, 1 FAIL. The single FAIL is a real spec violation: unknown public routes that don't match the proxy whitelist (e.g. /pagina-finta/) return HTTP 200 with the React SPA shell instead of 404.

integration_issues.issue: /pagina-finta/ (and any other unknown clean URL not in proxy EXACT/PREFIX list) returns HTTP 200 with the React SPA shell (index.html). Spec requires 404. Note: /episodi/non-esiste-xyz/ and /team/non-esiste/ correctly return 404 because the proxy routes them to the SSR backend which 404s. Only completely unknown top-level slugs fall through to the React dev server and get a 200.

test_results_detail.home_ssr:
PASS - / returns 200 with

UnoXdue

, , and JSON-LD @graph (Organization, WebSite, PodcastSeries). Body length > 500. Not a SPA shell.
test_results_detail.ssr_pages: PASS 11/11 - /, /il-podcast/, /episodi/, /interviste/, /pronostici/, /team/, /parlano-di-noi/, /collaborazioni/, /contatti/, /privacy/, /cookie/ all return 200 with H1 and canonical in source.
test_results_detail.menu_real_links: PASS 6/6 - header has real <a href="https://sportivo.preview.emergentagent.com/il-podcast/">, /episodi/, /interviste/, /pronostici/, /team/, /parlano-di-noi/. No '#' anchor links in header.
test_results_detail.mobile_menu: PASS - native <details>/<summary> present (works without JS). Confirmed in mobile viewport (390x844) screenshot.
test_results_detail.episodi_archive: PASS - /episodi/ lists 11 episode cards (giornate 29-38 puntate 1-10 + speciale-mondiali-2026-puntata-12), all with real <a href>.
test_results_detail.episode_detail: PASS - /episodi/serie-a-2025-2026-giornata-29-puntata-1/ returns H1 'Primo appuntamento UnoXdue: Serie A 2025/26, 29ª giornata' and breadcrumb contains 'Primo appuntamento'. Browser navigation to puntata-10 returned H1 'Decimo appuntamento UnoXdue: Serie A 2025/26, 38ª giornata'.

PASS - /episodi/serie-a-2025-2026-giornata-29-puntata-1/trascrizione/ returns 200 with multiple

paragraphs in source (browser test on puntata-10 showed 83

).
test_results_detail.404_routes: PARTIAL - /episodi/non-esiste-xyz/ -> 404 OK, /team/non-esiste/ -> 404 OK, but /pagina-finta/ -> 200 SPA shell (BUG).
test_results_detail.admin_spa: PASS - /admin loads React SPA showing login form ('UnoXdue admin Email Password Entra'). No login attempted as instructed.
test_results_detail.brand_spelling: PASS 11/11 - no 'Unoxdue' (wrong casing) found in any public page source. Always 'UnoXdue'.
test_results_detail.jsonld: PASS - home contains <script type="application/ld+json"> with Organization, WebSite, PodcastSeries entities.

action_items: Fix /pagina-finta/ (and any unknown clean URL not matching the proxy whitelist) to return HTTP 404. Options: (a) extend the proxy isPublic() to forward ALL non-asset, non-/admin, non-/api paths to the SSR backend so the FastAPI 404 handler is hit; or (b) ensure index.html served by the React dev server responds with a status 404 + 404 page for unmatched routes. Option (a) is preferred because in production the same reverse proxy mapping must also 404 unknown clean URLs.
critical_code_review_comments: frontend/src/setupProxy.js: the isPublic() whitelist (EXACT + PREFIX) silently makes any unknown clean URL (e.g. /pagina-finta/, /qualsiasi-cosa/) bypass SSR and fall through to the React dev server which always returns 200. This breaks the SEO 404 contract. Recommend: invert the policy — proxy EVERYTHING except /admin, /static, /api, /ws, /sockjs-node, /__, /manifest.json, /asset-manifest.json, /favicon.ico, hot-update; the backend will then 404 unknown SSR routes correctly. The current /episodi/<slug>/ behaviour (404 from SSR) is the desired model.

backend/templates/base.html: links in header/footer are emitted as absolute URLs (https://sportivo.preview.emergentagent.com/...). Functionally fine, but consider switching to root-relative paths to avoid hard dependency on the canonical host in HTML, simplifying future domain changes (e.g. unoxdue.net).

backend/server.py /seo/* routes: HEAD requests on / return 405 ('allow: GET'). FastAPI by default doesn't auto-implement HEAD for GET routes. Some crawlers (and some uptime monitors) probe with HEAD; consider allowing HEAD or letting the framework respond with an empty body for HEAD on these routes.

robots.txt route is whitelisted in setupProxy.js but EXACT does not include trailing slash; both /sitemap.xml and /robots.txt are correctly mapped to /api/<file>. Verify these endpoints exist in backend (not tested in this iteration).
test_credentials: Not used. Public-only testing as requested.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Public SSR suite is in /app/backend/tests/test_public_ssr.py and runs against REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL. All clean URLs are server-rendered HTML (verified in source via requests, not just rendered DOM). The only failure is /pagina-finta/ returning 200 instead of 404; this is due to frontend/src/setupProxy.js isPublic() whitelist not covering arbitrary unknown paths, so they fall through to the React dev server. Episode detail H1 format is '{Primo|Secondo|...|Decimo} appuntamento UnoXdue: Serie A 2025/26, {N}ª giornata'. Mobile menu uses native <details>/<summary> (no JS required). /admin still loads the React SPA login. Brand always 'UnoXdue' (no 'Unoxdue' anywhere).
rca of the issue: Root cause for /pagina-finta/ returning 200: frontend/src/setupProxy.js defines isPublic(pathname) returning true only when path is in EXACT set or starts with one of PREFIX ['/episodi','/interviste','/pronostici','/team']. Any other clean path returns false from isPublic, so http-proxy-middleware skips proxying and the request is served by the React dev server's catch-all (index.html, status 200). Reproduction: curl -sI https://sportivo.preview.emergentagent.com/pagina-finta/ -> HTTP/2 200 with React shell. Mitigation: change setupProxy.js to invert the rule (proxy everything except /admin, /static, /api, /ws, /sockjs-node, /__, /manifest.json, /asset-manifest.json, /favicon.ico, *hot-update*) so the FastAPI SSR backend gets the request and returns 404 for unknown slugs; or alternatively rewrite to /api/seo<path> with a backend wildcard catch-all that 404s.
